back: A field at the intersection of economics (game theory) and computer science (algorithms) that focuses on the algorithmic aspects of game-theoretic models and the strategic behavior of agents in complex computational systems.

front: What is the primary goal of studying mechanism design (or reverse game theory)?
back: To design games (or mechanisms) such that the system's desired outcome is achieved when self-interested agents act strategically to maximize their own utility.
A measure of how the efficiency of a system degrades due to the selfish behavior of its agents. It is the ratio between the worst-case Nash equilibrium outcome and the socially optimal outcome.
A solution concept of a game where no player can benefit by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.

front: What is the computational complexity challenge associated with Nash Equilibria?
back: Finding a Nash Equilibrium is known to be PPAD-complete for general games, a complexity class suggesting that a computationally efficient (polynomial-time) algorithm is unlikely to exist.
